metadata.title: Measure the Homework/Quiz Gap: Build the Bastani Test with Claude
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: The 17-point gap is not about the AI being bad — it's about which cognitive events happened during the session.

The Bastani RCT found students using AI for homework scored 17 points lower on the unassisted quiz. The mechanism: AI-delegation pattern (prompt, accept, move on) produces fluency without comprehension. The high-scoring pattern — prediction error before reading, verification after — builds durable skill because the brain encodes corrections, not confirmations.

claude "Walk through this algorithm step by step. Before explaining each step, I will predict what happens next. After your explanation, tell me if my prediction was right or wrong and why." < algorithm.md

props.command: claude "Walk through this algorithm step by step. Before explaining each step, I will predict what happens next. After your explanation, tell me if my prediction was right or wrong and why." < algorithm.md

beats.narration_text: bastani_test.py — generates two learning sessions (delegation vs. prediction-loop), runs comprehension quiz on both, displays performance differential.

props.code:
# generates two learning sessions and measures performance differential
import claude_session, quiz_engine

delegation = claude_session.run(mode='delegation', prompts=8)
prediction_loop = claude_session.run(mode='prediction_loop', cycles=8)

quiz_engine.score(delegation, label='delegation')
quiz_engine.score(prediction_loop, label='prediction-loop')
quiz_engine.diff()

beats.narration_text: Delegation session: 8 prompts, 0 prediction cycles, quiz score 61%. Prediction-loop session: 8 prompts, 8 prediction-verification cycles, quiz score 78%. Differential: 17 points. The prediction-loop session took 4 more minutes and produced 17 more correct answers per 100 questions.
Add third condition — explanation pairing (ask why a design decision was made, then verify).

props.command: claude "Add a third condition: explanation pairing. For each step, ask why the design decision was made, then verify the answer." < bastani_test.py

beats.narration_text: Explanation-pairing session: 8 prompts, 8 why-verification cycles, quiz score 81%. 20 points above delegation. The why-question forces active retrieval of prior knowledge — a different cognitive mechanism than prediction, but equally effective. Both outperform delegation by the same mechanism: the brain encodes what it gets wrong, not what it confirms.

The gap is reproducible by design. Delegation produces fluency — reading coherent output feels like mastery. Prediction error and explanation verification produce comprehension — because they require the brain to generate before comparing. One produces a session you remember reading. The other produces knowledge.
Next: build an AGENTS.md generator that tells Codex exactly what it needs to know about your project.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist plus Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
